seoTitle: How to Use Work Items and Work Types in Jira
metaDescription: Understand how work items and work types function in Jira's newer terminology so you can organize issues correctly.
intro: Jira has been shifting some of its terminology toward 'work items' and 'work types' as part of a broader move to unify issue tracking across teams. This guide covers what these terms mean in practice and how to use them.

steps.heading: Understand what a work item is
steps.body: A work item is essentially what used to be called an issue — a single trackable unit of work like a task, bug, or story.

steps.heading: Review available work types
steps.body: Check your project's configured work types, which define the different categories of work items you can create.

steps.heading: Create a work item with the right type
steps.body: When creating new work, choose the work type that best matches what you're tracking, whether that's a task, bug, or epic.

steps.heading: Customize work types if needed
steps.body: Admins can add or adjust work types to better fit how the team categorizes its work.

steps.heading: Use work types to filter and report
steps.body: Filter boards and reports by work type to see how different categories of work are progressing.

faq.question: Is a work item the same as an issue in Jira?
faq.answer: Yes, work item is newer terminology Jira has introduced that maps to what was previously called an issue.

faq.question: Do I need to change my existing project when this terminology updates?
faq.answer: No, existing projects and data continue to function the same; the change is primarily in labeling within the interface.

faq.question: Can I create custom work types?
faq.answer: Yes, similar to custom issue types, administrators can define new work types to match specific team needs.

faq.question: Does this terminology change affect the Jira API?
faq.answer: API behavior can vary by version, so it's worth checking Jira's documentation if you rely on integrations referencing issue types.
